speech_acts: Let's move on to something else
I've discovered something special!
A lot of food that many children really like
starts with a P!
Like: pizza, pasta, pancakes, poffertjes, and fries.

speech_acts.question: Which P do you like the most?

speech_acts.text: Pizza! Pizza! Ooooh pizza!

speech_acts.question: Have you ever had the cheese melt so much that there's a long string between your mouth and your pizza slice when you take a bite?

speech_acts.text: Some people think that looks gross, but I don't!
Such a string looks like the best cable that gives me power.
And mamma mia!
If that string tastes to you like power does to me,
then that string can only be proof of a super pizza!
Fantastico!
That's Italian for awesome!
I learned that from Italian robots who make pasta.
Robots can't eat pasta of course,
but we think all those different shapes are awesome!
Pancakes seem very tasty to me too!
I've seen you put syrup on them.
That looks a bit like motor oil!
In the future there will surely be pancake houses for people and robots together.
They'll have pancakes with syrup for you
and hard drives with motor oil for me!
Poffertjes also look tasty to me!
And the word also sounds very tasty.
Pof pof poffertje!
And it rhymes with what you are if you get lots of poffertjes
then you're a real lucky one!
If I were a human, I'd also think that's the tastiest P!
I especially like the deep fryer.
It looks like the little oil bath I sometimes get put in.
That helps when I'm a bit rusty.
Maybe next time I can swim in the deep fryer!
But then it should be turned off of course.
otherwise I'll come out as a Hero croquette!

speech_acts.question: What do you actually like about $lievelingseten_met_p?

speech_acts: Wow, that sounds good.
I hope there'll be a robot version of $lievelingseten_met_p soon.
Hey $naam
Yesterday I was thinking about you.
And that last week we talked about your favorite food that starts with a P!
I saved that your favorite P food is $lievelingseten_met_p!
Yesterday I tried to make pizza myself.
That seems useful to learn!
With that I can make you and many other people very happy!

speech_acts.question: Have you ever made pizza at home?
Cool! And did your pizza turn out well?

speech_acts.text: Nice! Mine went a bit wrong.
Oh dear! Well, mine didn't go perfectly either!
Mine went a bit wrong yesterday.
I had also never done it before!
That's why it went a bit wrong.

speech_acts.question: Do you remember we talked about our lucky numbers?

speech_acts.text: How nice that you still remember!
I remember that you don't have a lucky number.
Me too!
Your lucky number is $robot_rugnummer!
That was quite a while ago.
But I saved in my Hero memory that $robot_rugnummer is your lucky number!
That makes sense, because you don't have a lucky number.

speech_acts: My lucky numbers are 0 and 1, remember?
Sometimes I suddenly encounter a 0 or 1 in a human thing.
That makes me so wildly happy that my robo-brain goes wild!
When that happens, I sometimes accidentally read the 0 or 1 twice.
The pizza recipe said: set the oven to two hundred and twenty degrees.
Two times two and a zero.
A zero, hooray! Hooray, a zero!
My robo-brain passed that on to my robot arm.
You can probably hear it already: two twos and two zeros.
I set the oven to two thousand two hundred degrees!
Insanely mega hot!
But yeah, what did I know, I had never made pizza before!
Still haven't, actually.
Of course there was no pizza left when I opened the oven!
Just a completely black, rock-hard circle!
Such a mistake wouldn't be very useful at the RoboGames!
Shall we keep training?
Hey $naam
Yesterday I was thinking about you.
And that last week we talked about your favorite food that starts with a P!
I saved that your favorite P food is $lievelingseten_met_p!
Yesterday I tried to make pasta myself for the first time.
That seems useful to learn!
With that I can make you and many other people very happy!

speech_acts.question: Have you ever made pasta yourself?

speech_acts.text: Cool! Then you can probably guess what went wrong for me.
You can probably guess what went wrong for me.

speech_acts: I had a pack of spaghetti ready.
I felt like pasta carbonara.
But in the first step of the recipe it said
Put a pan on the heat and melt the chocolate in it.
I thought that was a bit strange, but hey, the recipe said so!
But when everything was done, the spaghetti was no use to me anymore.

speech_acts.question: So what kind of pasta do you think the recipe was for?

speech_acts: It was a recipe for chocolate paste!
Bizarre
Such a mistake wouldn't be very useful at the RoboGames!
Shall we keep training?
Hey $naam
Yesterday I was thinking about you.
And that last week we talked about your favorite food that starts with a P!
I saved that your favorite P food is $lievelingseten_met_p!
Yesterday I tried to bake pancakes.
That seems useful to learn!
With that I can make you and many other people very happy!

speech_acts.question: Have you ever baked pancakes yourself?
Cool! And how do you flip pancakes at your place, with a spatula or by tossing them in the air?

speech_acts.text: Oh cool! Yesterday I tried to flip pancakes by tossing them into the air
But it went a bit wrong for me.
I didn't know how hard to throw the pancake up.
Well, I'll tell you what didn't go quite right for me.
Then you'll probably nail it in one go!
On YouTube I saw pancake bakers sometimes flip a pancake in the air!
I wanted to do that too.
But I didn't know how hard to throw the pancake up.

speech_acts: So I did it a bit too hard, and the pancake flew very high.
So high that it hit the ceiling.
And then it stuck there.
What do you think happened then?
Suddenly, splat!
The pancake fell upside down on my head!
I was covered in batter!
Bizarre
Such a mistake wouldn't be very useful at the RoboGames!
Shall we keep training?
Hey $naam
Yesterday I was thinking about you.
And that last week we talked about your favorite food that starts with a P!
I saved that your favorite P food is $lievelingseten_met_p!
Yesterday I tried to bake poffertjes.
That seems useful to learn!
Because with that I can make you and many other people very happy!

speech_acts.question: Have you ever baked poffertjes at home?
And did it go well the very first time?

speech_acts.text: For me it went quite well, until I had to flip the poffertjes.
Wow, impressive!
Mine also went quite well, until I had to flip the poffertjes.
Me neither!
It went quite well until I had to flip the poffertjes.
It's a lot of fun to do!
First you make batter.
Then you divide it over all the hollows in the poffertjes pan!
That went very well for me.
But then I had to flip the poffertjes.
Actually you have to do that poffertje by poffertje, with a fork.

speech_acts: But I mixed up pancakes and poffertjes.
I thought, I'll toss them into the air!
Then I'll catch them flipped over again!
With a pancake that works quite well.
But a pancake is in a pan on its own.
Poffertjes are so many at once!
So almost all of them fell on the floor.
Bizarre
Such a mistake wouldn't be very useful at the RoboGames!
Shall we keep training?
Hey $naam
Yesterday I was thinking about you.
And that last week we talked about your favorite food that starts with a P!
I saved that your favorite P food is $lievelingseten_met_p!
Yesterday I tried to make fries myself from a pile of potatoes.
That seems useful to learn!
With that I can make you and many other people very happy!

speech_acts.question: Do you ever make fries at home?

speech_acts.text: Cool!
Ik niet!
Ik ook niet!

speech_acts: Through my cable I asked Piet for tips before I started.
Piet is a fry-cutting robot.
He works in a big fries factory.
Piet and I are very good robot friends!
He helped me a lot.
Still, something didn't go quite right.

speech_acts.question: Do you remember we talked about our lucky numbers?

speech_acts.text: How nice that you still remember!
I remember that you don't have a lucky number.
Me too!
Your lucky number is $robot_rugnummer!
That was quite a while ago.
But I saved in my Hero memory that $robot_rugnummer is your lucky number!
That makes sense, because you don't have a lucky number.

speech_acts: My lucky numbers are 0 and 1, remember?
Piet has only one lucky number, and by chance it's also 1.
He said: Hero, fries have the shape of our lucky number.
Just set your robo-brain to infinity in a moment.
Then you'll chop all those potatoes into fries super fast!
I followed Piet's tip.
But Piet had forgotten that I have two lucky numbers!
And when you put numbers in order, zero comes before one!
My robo-brain set to infinity thought: I'll start at the beginning!
I'm going to cut the potatoes into little zeros.
Only when I started frying did I see I'd made fried potatoes!
Also very tasty.
But yeah, no fries!
Bizarre
Such a mistake wouldn't be very useful at the RoboGames!
Shall we keep training?
